claim: LangSmith Sandboxes use hardware-virtualized microVMs for isolation, not containers alone; container-only isolation is insufficient for untrusted agent code
actionable: For production agent code execution, use hardware-virtualized sandboxes; do not rely on container isolation for security-critical workloads
source_title: Run Untrusted Agent Code with LangSmith Sandboxes

claim: Auth proxies in sandboxes enable network-level lockdown; agents cannot access unauthorized resources even if prompt-injected or malicious
actionable: Configure auth proxies with allowlists/denylists; keep credentials in proxy, not sandbox; block malicious MCPs at network layer
source_title: Run Untrusted Agent Code with LangSmith Sandboxes

claim: LangSmith Sandboxes achieve sub-1 second spin-up times enabling fast agent iteration; no pre-warming needed for thousands of sandboxes
actionable: Plan for fast sandbox startup; implement agent workflows that spin up and tear down sandboxes on-demand
source_title: Run Untrusted Agent Code with LangSmith Sandboxes

claim: Sandboxes support snapshot/restore for agent testing; agents can fork from a known state to explore multiple branches without data loss
actionable: Use sandbox snapshots before risky operations; fork to test multiple agent strategies from same starting point
source_title: Run Untrusted Agent Code with LangSmith Sandboxes

claim: Sandboxes enable long-running agent sessions with persistent state; no timeout limits and agents can pause/resume without losing files or context
actionable: Design agents for multi-hour sessions; use sandbox persistence for stateful workflows that span days
source_title: Run Untrusted Agent Code with LangSmith Sandboxes

claim: Multiple agents can share a single sandbox; enables multi-agent workflows with shared mutable state like a local computer
actionable: Architect multi-agent systems with shared sandboxes for coordination; design for concurrent access patterns
source_title: Run Untrusted Agent Code with LangSmith Sandboxes
